info.title: GR-Class Marine Backend API (ADMIN view)
info.description:
Production-grade API documentation for GR-Class Marine Backend.
Use role-specific docs: `/api-docs/admin`, `/api-docs/gm`, `/api-docs/tm`, `/api-docs/surveyor`, `/api-docs/client`,`/api-docs/public`


**Filtered view:** Only endpoints accessible to role **ADMIN**.

bearerAuth.description: JWT access token from login or refresh-token. Use in header: Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>

example.message: Validation failed. Please correct the highlighted fields.

ActivityRequestConvertToJobRequest.description:
Same shape as job create, but only `certificate_type_id` is required.
`vessel_id`, `target_port`, `target_date`, and `reason` default from the
activity request when omitted.

certificate_type_id.description: Certificate type for the new job (required at conversion).

reason.description: Job reason; defaults to requested_service + description.

message.example: Activity request converted to job successfully

LoginResponse.description: Returns user and both tokens. Use accessToken for API calls (Bearer header); use refreshToken only at POST /auth/refresh-token. Store tokens securely.

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT for API auth. Send in header: Authorization Bearer <accessToken>

refreshToken.description: Long-lived JWT; use only to get new access token via POST /auth/refresh-token

RefreshTokenRequest.description: Send refresh token in body (refreshToken or token). Cookie refreshToken is also accepted.

refreshToken.description: Refresh token from login/register or previous refresh

accessToken.description: New short-lived access token for API calls

refreshToken.description: New long-lived refresh token; store and use for next refresh

code.description: Stable template code (used as a stable reference).

description.description: Short description of what the template covers.

certificate_type_id.description:
Certificate type this template applies to. When activated, the
active template for this certificate type drives surveyor checklists.

sections.description: Ordered list of sections with their question items.

text.description: Question text shown to the surveyor

template_files.description:
S3 keys of attached reference DOCX/PDF files (each obtained from
`GET /api/v1/checklist-templates/get-upload-url`).

uploadUrl.description: Pre-signed S3 PUT URL. Upload the file directly with this URL.

fileKey.description: S3 object key. Send this back to the API afterwards.

ChecklistTemplateUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /api/v1/checklist-templates/{id}`. All fields are optional —
send only what you want to change.

For attached document keys you have THREE choices (mutually exclusive
between `template_files` and the `add_*`/`remove_*` pair):
  • `template_files`         — full replace of the entire array
  • `add_template_files`     — append these keys
  • `remove_template_files`  — drop these keys

Structural edits (`name`, `code`, `sections`, `certificate_type_id`,
`metadata`) require the template to be in `DRAFT`. File-key edits are
allowed at any status.

template_files.description: Full replace of the attached-document key list.

add_template_files.description:
S3 keys to append (de-duplicated). Use this to "add a new file on
top" without losing the existing ones.

remove_template_files.description: S3 keys to drop from the existing list.

signed_checklist_files.description:
Resolved (signed) HTTPS URLs of the full signed-checklist scan
documents. Empty array when none are attached.

template_files.description:
Resolved (signed) HTTPS URLs of the reference/blank checklist
template documents (DOCX/PDF) attached to the active checklist
template for this job's certificate type.
Empty array when no active template exists or it has no attached files.

template.description:
Minimal metadata for the active checklist template used to provide
`template_files`. Null when no active template exists for the job's
certificate type.

DashboardResponse.description: Role-specific dashboard data. Structure varies by role (ADMIN, GM, TM, TO, SURVEYOR, CLIENT)

source_activity_request_id.description: Set when the job was created from an activity request conversion

source_activity_request.description: Present on job detail when converted from an activity request

payment.description: Optional payment object (only processed for ADMIN/GM). Instantly creates an invoice.

signed_checklist_files.description: Resolved (signed) HTTPS URLs of surveyor uploaded signed checklist scans.

ActivityPlannings.description: List of checklist items and surveyor findings for this job.

uploaded_documents.description: Job supporting documents with signed download URLs.

payment_date.description: Set when invoice is fully paid (detail responses).

paid_at.description: Full settlement date; null until payment_status is PAID (list).

amount_collected.description: Total collected via advance + partial payments

type.description: Use ADVANCE for upfront payments before work begins, PARTIAL_PAYMENT for installments during/after work

certificate_type_id.description: ID of the associated certificate type.

certificate_term.description: If set, this template is used only for that term.

template_file_url.description:
S3 key returned by
`GET /api/v1/certificate-templates/get-upload-url` (after the file
has been uploaded to that pre-signed URL).

variables.description: Variable names this template expects at render time.

CertificateTemplateResponse.description: A DOCX template (referenced by S3 key) used to render certificates.

template_file_url.description:
S3 key for the .docx template (e.g.
`certificate-templates/1714000000000_iopp_v1.docx`). This is what
`getUploadUrl` returned as `fileKey`.

variables.description: Variable names this template expects to receive at render time.

CertificateTemplateUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /api/v1/certificate-templates/{id}`. All fields optional —
send only what you want to change. To swap the DOCX, send the new S3
key as `template_file_url`.

fcmToken.description: The unique device token generated by the Firebase SDK.

source_activity_request_id.description: Back-reference to the originating activity request

rejection_reason.description: Rejection text, or `N/A` when not rejected

ActivityRequestVesselBlock.description: Flat vessel summary for activity request screens (all fields `N/A` when no vessel linked).

post.summary: Convert approved activity request to job
post.description:
**Step 3 — Formal job.** Requires status `APPROVED`.
Only `certificate_type_id` is required in the body; vessel, port, date, and reason
default from the activity request. Blank strings in the body are ignored.
Mandatory job documents are **not** required at convert (upload on job later).

example.message: Activity request converted to job successfully

job.reason: Annual hull inspection — Request survey before Rotterdam departure

get.description:
List certificates with strict RBAC and ownership filtering.
Use `expiring_within_days` to filter certificates expiring soon (defaults to VALID when status omitted).
`status_counts` returns every certificate status with count 0 where none match (same filters as list except `status`).
**Access:**
- **ADMIN / GM / TM / TO:** All certificates.
- **CLIENT:** Only certificates for their own company vessels.

parameters.name: expiring_within_days
parameters.description: Return VALID certificates expiring within N days (default 30 when used).

get.summary: Get presigned URL for certificate upload
get.description:
Returns a presigned S3 URL for uploading external certificates. ADMIN, GM, TM only.

**IMPORTANT:** When performing the `PUT` request to the returned `uploadUrl`, you **MUST NOT** include the `Authorization` (Bearer token) header. Presigned URLs already contain authentication in the query parameters. Including both will cause an `InvalidArgument` error from S3.

delete.description:
**Soft-deactivates** a certificate type by setting its status to `INACTIVE`.
This is a safe alternative to hard-delete — the type remains in the database
for historical reference but can no longer be assigned to new jobs.

**Guards before deactivation:**
- Cannot deactivate if any certificate using this type is still active (not REVOKED/EXPIRED)
- Cannot deactivate if any job linked to this type is still pending (not CERTIFIED/REJECTED/CANCELLED)

**Role:** ADMIN only

example.message: Certificate type deactivated successfully.

409.description:
Conflict — cannot deactivate because:
- Certificate type is already inactive
- Active certificates still reference this type
- Active jobs still reference this type

put.summary: Update template (full replace OR add/remove file keys)
put.description:
Update template fields. Three independent ways to manage attached
document keys are supported:

| Field | Effect |
|---|---|
| `template_files` | Full replace of the entire array |
| `add_template_files` | Append these keys (de-duplicated) |
| `remove_template_files` | Remove these specific keys |

`add_*` and `remove_*` can be combined in one call, but **cannot** be
combined with the full-replace `template_files`.

Structural edits (`name`, `code`, `sections`, `certificate_type_id`,
`metadata`) require the template to be in `DRAFT` status — clone the
template instead for finalized versions. File-key edits are allowed at
any status.

addFiles.summary: Append a newly-uploaded reference doc (any status)

addAndRemove.summary: Swap one file for another (any status)

example.message: string

400.description:
Either (a) you tried to mix full-replace `template_files` with
`add_*`/`remove_*`, or (b) you tried to make a structural edit on a
non-DRAFT template.

get.description:
Returns the checklist items for a job **plus** the resolved (signed)
HTTPS URLs of any full signed-checklist scan documents that were
attached to the underlying survey.

All `file_url` fields and `signed_checklist_files` entries are guaranteed
to be either fully-qualified HTTPS URLs or `null` — raw S3 keys are never
returned to the client.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a flag administration record.

**Guards before deletion:**
- Returns **409** if the flag is currently assigned to one or more vessels
- Returns **409** if the flag is referenced in any certificates

If the flag is in use, reassign or update those records first, then retry.

**Role:** ADMIN only

Message describes exactly how many vessels or certificates reference it.

get.summary: Get presigned S3 upload URL for job documents
get.description:
> **Deprecated** – Use `GET /api/v1/documents/get-upload-url` instead.

Generates a signed URL for uploading files directly to S3. 
Returns the `uploadUrl` (where to PUT the file) and the `fileKey` 
(to be used as `file_url` in subsequent job registration calls).

**Roles:** CLIENT, ADMIN, GM, TM, SURVEYOR

Returns a paginated list of job requests. Visible set depends on caller role:
- **CLIENT** – Only jobs for their fleet (vessel_id filtered to client).
- **SURVEYOR** – Only jobs assigned to them (`assigned_surveyor_id = me`).
- **ADMIN / GM / TM / TO** – All jobs; defaults to last 30 days
  unless explicit filters are supplied.

Supply `status` as a **comma-separated** string to filter by multiple statuses,
e.g. `?status=ASSIGNED,SURVEY_AUTHORIZED`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status (single value or comma-separated list)

parameters.name: created_from
parameters.description: ISO date string – start of creation window

parameters.name: created_to
parameters.description: ISO date string – end of creation window

parameters.name: recent_days
parameters.description:
(Internal roles only) Shortcut to filter to last N days when no other date
filter is given. Default is 30.

example.message: Validation failed. Please correct the highlighted fields.
Validation failed. Please correct the highlighted fields.

post.description:
Opens a new certification job request in **CREATED** status.
- **CLIENT** – Can only submit for vessels that belong to their account.
- **ADMIN / GM** – Can submit for any vessel.

If the selected `certificate_type_id` has mandatory required documents
(`CertificateRequiredDocument` where `is_mandatory=true`), each must be
included in `uploaded_documents`; otherwise the request will be rejected
with a `400` listing the missing documents.

400.description: Validation error or missing mandatory documents

message.example: Missing mandatory documents for job creation.

example.message: Missing mandatory documents for job creation.

Returns full job detail including vessel, certificate type, status history,
uploaded documents, reschedule records, and certificate (with signed URL if
applicable).
- **CLIENT** – Own fleet only.
- **SURVEYOR** – Only if assigned to them.
- **ADMIN / GM / TM / TO** – Any job.

put.description:
**Transition:** `CREATED → DOCUMENT_VERIFIED` (when all docs approved)

Technical Officer (or Admin/GM) reviews uploaded documents for validity and completeness.

**Two flows:**
1. **Approve all** (`approved: true` or empty body) — Marks all documents as
   APPROVED and transitions the job to `DOCUMENT_VERIFIED`.
2. **Reject specific docs** (`approved: false`) — Marks listed documents as
   REJECTED with reasons. Job stays in `CREATED`. Client is notified to
   re-upload the rejected documents.

If mandatory documents are missing (not uploaded at all), returns `400`
regardless of the `approved` flag.

**Roles:** ADMIN, GM, TO

approved.description: true = all docs valid, false = some docs invalid

rejected_documents.description: Required when approved=false. List of invalid documents with reasons.

document_id.description: ID of the JobDocument to reject

rejected_documents.reason: Certificate expired, please upload a valid one.

200.description:
Documents processed. If approved, job moves to DOCUMENT_VERIFIED.
If rejected, job stays in CREATED and client is notified.

example.message: All documents verified successfully.

400.description:
Job not in CREATED state, mandatory documents missing,
or `rejected_documents` array is empty when `approved=false`.

and sends a push notification to the surveyor.

put.description:
**Terminal transition** – moves job to `REJECTED` permanently.

Role-specific constraints:
- **ADMIN / GM** – Any status before `FINALIZED` or `CERTIFIED`.
- **TM** – Only in `ASSIGNED`, `SURVEY_DONE`, or `REVIEWED` states.

**Roles:** ADMIN, GM, TM

put.description:

- **TM** – Follows same status restrictions as rejection.

put.description:
Updates the `priority` field of a job. Creates an audit log entry with
the old and new values plus an optional reason.

Priority does **not** affect the job workflow or status — it is a
management flag only, used to sort / filter jobs in dashboards.

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `LOW` | Non-urgent, can wait |
| `NORMAL` | Standard priority (default) |
| `HIGH` | Requires prompt attention |
| `URGENT` | Must be actioned immediately |

**Roles:** ADMIN, GM, TM

post.description:
Client or staff uploads additional documents or replaces existing ones.
Only allowed while job is in `CREATED` status.

This endpoint handles both initial uploads and replacement of rejected 
documents. If a `required_document_id` is provided for a document that
already exists, its `file_url` will be updated and its `verification_status`
will be reset to `PENDING`.

Notifies TO team that new or updated documents are available for review.

**Roles:** CLIENT, ADMIN, GM, TM

required_document_id.description: ID from CertificateRequiredDocument (if this is a mandatory doc)

custom_document_name.description: Name of the document (if it is an additional/custom document)

file_url.description: S3/CDN URL of the uploaded file

example.message: Documents uploaded successfully.

400.description:
Job not in CREATED state, document already exists for this requirement,
or missing required fields.

put.description:
Client or staff replaces a previously rejected document with a new version.
Only documents with `verification_status = REJECTED` can be re-uploaded.
The new version is created as a separate record (PENDING) to maintain
audit trail of the rejected original.

Only allowed while the job is in `CREATED` status.

**Roles:** CLIENT, ADMIN, GM, TM

parameters.name: documentId
parameters.description: UUID of the rejected JobDocument to replace

file_url.description: S3/CDN URL of the replacement file

200.description: Document re-uploaded successfully (new version in PENDING state)

example.message: Document re-uploaded successfully.

as PAID when total collected >= invoice amount. **ADMIN, GM, TM, TO only.**

post.description:
Create a new DOCX template (referenced by an S3 key) used for
generating certificates.

To attach the DOCX file:
  1. Call `GET /api/v1/certificate-templates/get-upload-url`.
  2. PUT the file to the returned `uploadUrl`.
  3. Send the returned `fileKey` as `template_file_url` here.
